info.name: 006 LEADS_MARKETING_SITE
info.description:
All public /leads/* surface consumed by the vianda-home marketing site. Organized by frontend consumer (one collection per consumer), not per endpoint group.

Folders:
- Zipcode metrics (no auth, no reCAPTCHA) — navbar coverage probe.
- Country selectors (no auth, no reCAPTCHA) — /leads/countries, /leads/supplier-countries. Response is LeadsCountriesResponseSchema envelope: {countries, suggested_country_code}. Cache-Control: private, no-store (kitchen #217 — breaking change from former array response).
- country_code contract — /leads/plans, /leads/restaurants, /leads/featured-restaurant (required → 400; unsupported → [] or null, 200).
- Admin override on market status (guardrails) — PUT /admin/markets/{id} activation/deactivation rules. Requires super admin.

Contracts:
- docs/api/marketing_site/LEADS_COVERAGE_CHECKER.md
- docs/api/b2c_client/feedback_from_client/RESTAURANTS_BY_ZIPCODE.md
- Cron follow-up: docs/plans/market-status-cron.md

Shared auth: Login Super Admin is idempotent — if `superAdminAuthToken` is already set as a Postman global (e.g. via Newman --globals from a seed run), the login request is skipped. See docs/postman/guidelines/LEADS_COLLECTION_CONVENTIONS.md.

item.name: Cities endpoint (/leads/cities)
item.description:
GET /api/v1/leads/cities — flat city list (default + supplier audience) and coverage mode. No auth, reCAPTCHA bypassed in dev. Ported from app/tests/routes/test_leads.py::TestLeadsCitiesEndpoint.

Divergence note — caching behavior: the original pytest patches time.time() to verify TTL enforcement. End-to-end TTL is unverifiable at the HTTP layer without clock manipulation. The two requests below verify response-shape stability across two sequential calls and that both return the same first city; TTL enforcement itself is not asserted.

request.description: Default mode (no mode param). Response: LeadsCitiesResponseSchema with a 'cities' array of strings. Port of test_default_mode_returns_flat_city_list.

item.name: GET /leads/cities (default mode, 2nd call) — shape stable + same first city

exec: pm.test('First city is consistent across sequential calls (cache stability)', () => {
// NOTE: TTL enforcement (time.time() patching) is unverifiable end-to-end.
// Two sequential calls verifying shape + same first-city is the HTTP-layer proxy.

request.description: Second sequential call to /leads/cities. Verifies shape stability and that the first city is the same (cache hit). TTL enforcement itself is not asserted end-to-end. Port of test_caching_behavior (diverged).
audience=supplier still returns flat city list (backward compatibility unchanged). Port of test_supplier_audience_still_returns_flat_list.

mode=coverage returns [{city, restaurant_count}] objects. Service excludes cities with zero active plate coverage (restaurant_count >= 1). Port of test_coverage_mode_returns_city_with_count_objects + test_coverage_mode_excludes_zero_count_cities.
mode=coverage with an unknown/unsupported country returns empty array (not an error). Port of test_coverage_mode_empty_country_returns_empty_list.

item.name: Markets has_active_kitchens field (/leads/markets)
item.description:
GET /api/v1/leads/markets — has_active_kitchens field assertions. No auth. Ported from app/tests/routes/test_leads.py::TestLeadsMarketsHasActiveKitchens.

Divergence note — archived-plate false case: the original pytest mocks get_coverage_flags_for_markets to return an empty set and asserts has_active_kitchens=false. End-to-end this depends on live DB state (all plates archived for a specific country). The Postman test instead verifies that has_active_kitchens is present and is a boolean in supplier mode, without asserting a specific true/false for a specific country.

exec: pm.test('No market_id in response (private field excluded)', () => {
console.log('No active markets in DB — skipping has_active_kitchens assertions.');

request.description: Default (customer) mode: all returned markets have passed the coverage filter, so has_active_kitchens is always true. market_id is excluded from the public response. Port of test_customer_mode_has_active_kitchens_true.

exec: // mocks is_global_market to exclude XG. End-to-end, XG exclusion depends on the
// market_id comparison working correctly with the DB-returned type. We verify the
// field shape rather than asserting XG exclusion, which is a unit-level concern.
// NOTE: asserting specific true/false per country is not reliable end-to-end
// because it depends on live DB state (archived vs active plates).
// The test verifies the field is present and boolean for all returned markets.

request.description: Supplier audience: returns all non-global markets with has_active_kitchens reflecting coverage. Field is true when the market has active restaurant/plate/kitchen-day chain, false otherwise. Global Marketplace (XG) is excluded. Divergence: the specific false-for-archived-plates case is not asserted end-to-end (requires DB state with all plates archived). Port of test_supplier_mode_has_active_kitchens_reflects_coverage + test_supplier_mode_archived_plate_yields_false (diverged).

exec: pm.test('Every market has expected public fields', () => {
pm.test('locale is derived from language + country_code (e.g. es-AR)', () => {

request.description: Batch check: all markets in the supplier response carry the full set of public fields and locale is correctly derived (language-COUNTRY format). Port of test_supplier_mode_has_active_kitchens_reflects_coverage (batch assertion part).

item.name: Admin override on market status (guardrails)
item.description: Exercises the admin override validation added to PUT /admin/markets/{id}. Requires a super admin token and at least one non-global market in the DB.

exec: // Skip when a token is already provided via Newman --globals or set by a prior run.
postman.setNextRequest('Discover a non-global market + its coverage status');

request.description: Skipped when superAdminAuthToken already exists as a Postman global. See docs/postman/guidelines/LEADS_COLLECTION_CONVENTIONS.md for the seed-collection / Newman --globals pattern.

item.name: Discover a non-global market + its coverage status
PUT /admin/markets/{id} status=active — expect 400 when market has no coverage OR 200 if it does

exec: pm.test('400 detail mentions Cannot activate', () => pm.expect((typeof body.detail === 'string' ? body.detail : (body.detail && body.detail.message) || '')).to.include('Cannot activate'));

pm.test('400 detail explains coverage requirement', () => pm.expect((typeof body.detail === 'string' ? body.detail : (body.detail && body.detail.message) || '')).to.match(/plate|coverage|kitchen-day/));

request.description: Admin guardrail: `status='active'` is refused when the market has no active restaurant → plate → plate_kitchen_days chain. The 30-day forward window tightening is deferred to the cron (`docs/plans/market-status-cron.md`).

item.name: PUT /admin/markets/{id} status=inactive (no confirm) — 409 if coverage, 200 otherwise

exec: pm.test('409 detail mentions active plate coverage', () => pm.expect((typeof body.detail === 'string' ? body.detail : (body.detail && body.detail.message) || '')).to.match(/active plate coverage|confirm_deactivate/));

pm.test('409 detail names confirm_deactivate as the unlock', () => pm.expect((typeof body.detail === 'string' ? body.detail : (body.detail && body.detail.message) || '')).to.include('confirm_deactivate'));

request.description: Deactivation warning: when the market has active plate coverage, the backend refuses without `confirm_deactivate: true`.
Deactivation succeeds when the second-confirm flag is set.

exec: console.warn('Could not restore original status — likely the admin-activation guardrail fired. Market left inactive.');

variable.description: Set by Login Super Admin, or pre-seeded via Newman --globals.
Canonical name across all leads collections. Any non-empty string when RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY is empty locally (verify_recaptcha short-circuits); real reCAPTCHA v3 token in staging/prod.
Retired (kitchen #217): ETags no longer sent on /leads/countries (Cache-Control is now private, no-store). Kept as placeholder to avoid breaking variable references in older runs.
First country code returned by /leads/countries — used to assert /leads/supplier-countries is a superset.
Non-global market UUID discovered at run time.
Original status captured before mutation; restored at the end.
'true' | 'false' — inferred from whether the unconfirmed deactivation returned 409.
